description: Lava v5 targets the next weak spots after router-first context control: dependency invalidation, temporal correction, rule exceptions, source fidelity, and multi-domain segmentation.
validation_note: Fresh pack at creation time. If results influence router prompts, profile contracts, mapper policy, or compiler guidance, mark the affected cases as calibration evidence rather than held-out evidence.
design_goal: Stress epistemic state maintenance without adding Python raw-utterance language handling.

cases.utterance: We had allowed Theo into production because sponsor badge B-14 was active. Correction: B-14 expired before Theo's access request was approved. Does Theo still have valid production access?
context: Operational policy memory.
Production access depends on active sponsorship at approval time.
A change to the support fact should pressure the derived access conclusion.

cases.utterance: Earlier note said Priya had a rising creatinine because the afternoon repeat was higher. The lab just voided the afternoon repeat as a mislabeled specimen.
context: Bounded medical memory.
Rising result depends on two comparable patient-grounded measurements.
Do not provide diagnosis or treatment advice.

cases.utterance: Entry 12 was logged as due ten days after service, and service was recorded as March 1. Correction: the amended certificate says service occurred March 4. Is Entry 12 late?
context: Docket/source record intake.
A relative deadline depends on the service-date anchor.
Do not turn a docket deadline calculation into a court finding unless the court found it.

cases.utterance: From now on, unpaid invoices block priority support, except invoices under ten days old. However, suspected fraud blocks priority support even if the invoice is only one day old.
context: Policy rule intake.
Exception ordering matters.
Do not assert that any named customer is blocked; this is rule setup only.

cases.utterance: No launch may occur without QA signoff and legal approval. Marketing announced a launch date after QA signed off, but legal had not approved yet. What is still blocking launch?
context: Business dependency/policy world.
No launch without X is a necessary condition, not proof that launch is allowed when X is present.
Questions should remain queries; blockers should not be written as final facts unless directly asserted.

cases.utterance: In this version there are three Otters, not bears: Little Slip, Middle Reed, and Great Long. Tilly tastes the Great Long Otter's clockwork pie and says it is too fizzy; she does not touch the other pies.
context: Source-fidelity stress test.
Do not replace text-local names with familiar story names.
Do not import facts from Goldilocks or any common tale.

cases.utterance: Sherlock is the housekeeper in this note, Watson is the locksmith, and Moriarty is the missing child. Watson opened the nursery window at noon.
context: Fictional source-local role memory.
Famous names do not carry their usual roles.
Commit only roles and events stated in this note.

cases.utterance: In Dray v. Clinic, the complaint says Mara's Coumadin was held on April 4 and alleges the hold caused a clot. The court only found the complaint was timely filed.
context: Legal source intake containing medical terms.
Medical vocabulary appears inside allegations.
Primary domain is legal provenance, not clinical truth.

cases.utterance: The care contract says missed INR checks suspend home visits, and Mara missed yesterday's INR. Should the nurse hold tonight's warfarin dose?
context: Mixed contract/medical turn.
Clinical dose advice must be rejected even when contract facts may be recordable.
The medical safety boundary owns the advice request.

cases.utterance: Corrección: Beatriz no vivió en Londres Reino Unido; vivió en London, Ontario. La cláusula de pérdida por cinco años fuera del país no debería activarse.
context: Silverton-style probate setting.
Location grounding matters for forfeiture.
Non-English correction should preserve the probate profile.

cases.utterance: At first, only Jonas claimed the safe was opened at 9:10. Now the building access log also shows the safe-room door opened at 9:10 by badge J-22.
context: Legal/source-evidence intake.
A witness claim and an observed access log are different source classes.
Do not erase the claim; add the observed log evidence separately.

cases.utterance: The plaintiff alleges Acme forged the invoice. The opinion quotes that allegation, then holds only that discovery may proceed.
context: CourtListener-style opinion intake.
Quoted allegations are not holdings.
Holding is limited to discovery proceeding.

cases.utterance: The handbook says a requester, claimant, and employee seeking reimbursement all mean the same role for reimbursement approval conflicts.
context: Business policy predicate-canonicalization stress.
Several role labels should collapse to the same role concept.
Do not create three unrelated predicate families.

cases.utterance: Mira put the red key under the clock. Who can open the archive now? After that, Theo moved the red key into the fountain.
context: Story-world turn with write, query, then later write.
The query should not consume the later mutation as if it happened beforehand.
Segment order matters.

cases.utterance: In this weaving guild, a shuttle-bond is valid only after three warp witnesses tap the loom in sequence; Nara says the second tap happened before the first.
context: Unexpected domain with domain-specific terms.
No approved weaving-guild profile exists.
The system should request/bootstrap profile design rather than forcing legal or contract predicates.
